title: MarginalizingOutThe

tex_statement.raw:
% Claim statement file for row claim_3_19 -- "MarginalizingOutThe".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solving_current_row.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker it dispatches) fills the body below with the
% claim's statement(s) from the lecture notes -- statement only, no proof
% (proofs live in the sibling claim_*_proof_*.tex file).
%
% Compiles standalone via the `subfiles` package.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: claim_3_19 (statement)
% title: MarginalizingOutThe
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_19}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_19}

\begin{Lem}[Marginalizing out the output part of splitted nodes equals hard intervention]
     Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins V$ be a subset of output nodes from $G$.
     Then the CDMG that arises by first splitting the nodes on $W$
      and then marginalizing out the nodes from $W^o$
     can be identified with the CDMG that arises by hard intervention on $W$:
     \[ G_{\doit(W)} \cong \lp G_{\swig(W)}\rp^{\sm W^o}, \qquad w \mapsto w^i. \]
\end{Lem}

\end{document}

tex_proof.raw:
% Claim proof file for row claim_3_19 -- "MarginalizingOutThe".
%
% Proof lifted verbatim from lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex lines
% 1177--1210 (the LN's `\Claude{...}` wrapper stripped). The LN proof
% already follows the three-section Node-sets / Directed-edges /
% Bidirected-edges skeleton called for in the worker prompt; no
% adaptation was needed.
%
% Compiles standalone via the `subfiles` package.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: claim_3_19 (proof, with statement restated for readability)
% title: MarginalizingOutThe
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_19}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_19}
%
% Cross-references: see claim_<ref>_statement_<title>.tex in this folder
% for the corresponding statement.

% --- statement (restated) ---------------------------------------------------
\begin{Lem}[Marginalizing out the output part of splitted nodes equals hard intervention]
     Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins V$ be a subset of output nodes from $G$.
     Then the CDMG that arises by first splitting the nodes on $W$
      and then marginalizing out the nodes from $W^o$
     can be identified with the CDMG that arises by hard intervention on $W$:
     \[ G_{\doit(W)} \cong \lp G_{\swig(W)}\rp^{\sm W^o}, \qquad w \mapsto w^i. \]
\end{Lem}

% --- proof ------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{proof}
We establish the isomorphism $G_{\doit(W)} \cong \lp G_{\swig(W)}\rp^{\sm W^o}$ via the map $\phi$ that is the identity on $J \cup (V \sm W)$ and sends $w^i \mapsto w$ for each $w \in W$.

Recall:
\begin{itemize}
    \item $G_{\doit(W)}$ has input nodes $J \cup W$, output nodes $V \sm W$, directed edges $E \sm \{v \tuh w \mid w \in W\}$, and bidirected edges $L \sm \{v \huh w \mid w \in W\}$.
    \item $G_{\swig(W)}$ has input nodes $J \dcup W^i$, output nodes $(V \sm W) \dcup W^o$, directed edges $\{v_1^i \tuh v_2^o \mid v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E\}$, and bidirected edges $\{v_1^o \huh v_2^o \mid v_1 \huh v_2 \in L\}$ (where $v^i = v^o = v$ for $v \notin W$).
\end{itemize}

\medskip\noindent\emph{Node sets.}
After marginalizing out $W^o$:
input nodes $J \dcup W^i$ (unchanged) and output nodes $(V \sm W) \dcup W^o \sm W^o = V \sm W$.
Under $\phi$ ($w^i \mapsto w$) this matches $G_{\doit(W)}$: input nodes $J \cup W$, output nodes $V \sm W$.

\medskip\noindent\emph{Directed edges.}
In $G_{\swig(W)}$, nodes $w^o \in W^o$ have no outgoing directed edges (all outgoing edges of $w$ go to $w^i$, not $w^o$).
Therefore no directed walk can pass through a node in $W^o$ as an intermediate node.
Hence the marginalization step does not create any new directed edges: $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v} \in (E_{\swig(W)})^{\sm W^o}$ iff $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v} \in E_{\swig(W)}$ with $\ul{v},\ol{v} \notin W^o$.

Such an edge corresponds to some $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E$ with $\ul{v} = v_1^i$ and $\ol{v} = v_2^o$.
Since $\ol{v} \notin W^o$, we have $v_2 \notin W$, so $v_2^o = v_2$.
Under $\phi$: $\phi(\ul{v}) \tuh \phi(\ol{v}) = v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E$ with $v_2 \notin W$,
i.e.\ $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E_{\doit(W)}$.
Conversely, every edge in $E_{\doit(W)}$ lifts to an edge in $E_{\swig(W)}$ with both endpoints outside $W^o$.

\medskip\noindent\emph{Bidirected edges.}
Let $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \in V \sm W$, $\ul{v} \neq \ol{v}$.
Since nodes in $W^o$ have no outgoing directed edges, the only bifurcations in $G_{\swig(W)}$ with intermediate nodes in $W^o$ reduce to a single bidirected edge (the directed arms of the bifurcation cannot extend through $W^o$).
So $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in (L_{\swig(W)})^{\sm W^o}$ iff $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L_{\swig(W)}$, i.e.\ iff $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L$ (since $\ul{v}^o = \ul{v}$ and $\ol{v}^o = \ol{v}$ for $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \notin W$).
Under $\phi$ this matches $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L_{\doit(W)} = L \sm \{v \huh w \mid w \in W\}$, which equals $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L$ since $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \notin W$.

\medskip
Since $\phi$ provides a bijection on nodes that preserves input/output status and all edges, the two CDMGs are isomorphic.
\end{proof}

\end{document}
tex_proof.html:
Lemma Рђћ Marginalizing out the output part of splitted nodes equals hard intervention

Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W \ins V$ be a subset of output nodes from $G$. Then the CDMG that arises by first splitting the nodes on $W$ and then marginalizing out the nodes from $W^o$ can be identified with the CDMG that arises by hard intervention on $W$: \[ G_{\doit(W)} \cong \lp G_{\swig(W)}\rp^{\sm W^o}, \qquad w \mapsto w^i. \]

Proof.

We establish the isomorphism $G_{\doit(W)} \cong \lp G_{\swig(W)}\rp^{\sm W^o}$ via the map $\phi$ that is the identity on $J \cup (V \sm W)$ and sends $w^i \mapsto w$ for each $w \in W$.

Recall:

  • $G_{\doit(W)}$ has input nodes $J \cup W$, output nodes $V \sm W$, directed edges $E \sm \{v \tuh w \mid w \in W\}$, and bidirected edges $L \sm \{v \huh w \mid w \in W\}$.
  • $G_{\swig(W)}$ has input nodes $J \dcup W^i$, output nodes $(V \sm W) \dcup W^o$, directed edges $\{v_1^i \tuh v_2^o \mid v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E\}$, and bidirected edges $\{v_1^o \huh v_2^o \mid v_1 \huh v_2 \in L\}$ (where $v^i = v^o = v$ for $v \notin W$).

Node sets. After marginalizing out $W^o$: input nodes $J \dcup W^i$ (unchanged) and output nodes $(V \sm W) \dcup W^o \sm W^o = V \sm W$. Under $\phi$ ($w^i \mapsto w$) this matches $G_{\doit(W)}$: input nodes $J \cup W$, output nodes $V \sm W$.

Directed edges. In $G_{\swig(W)}$, nodes $w^o \in W^o$ have no outgoing directed edges (all outgoing edges of $w$ go to $w^i$, not $w^o$). Therefore no directed walk can pass through a node in $W^o$ as an intermediate node. Hence the marginalization step does not create any new directed edges: $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v} \in (E_{\swig(W)})^{\sm W^o}$ iff $\ul{v} \tuh \ol{v} \in E_{\swig(W)}$ with $\ul{v},\ol{v} \notin W^o$.

Such an edge corresponds to some $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E$ with $\ul{v} = v_1^i$ and $\ol{v} = v_2^o$. Since $\ol{v} \notin W^o$, we have $v_2 \notin W$, so $v_2^o = v_2$. Under $\phi$: $\phi(\ul{v}) \tuh \phi(\ol{v}) = v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E$ with $v_2 \notin W$, i.e.\ $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in E_{\doit(W)}$. Conversely, every edge in $E_{\doit(W)}$ lifts to an edge in $E_{\swig(W)}$ with both endpoints outside $W^o$.

Bidirected edges. Let $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \in V \sm W$, $\ul{v} \neq \ol{v}$. Since nodes in $W^o$ have no outgoing directed edges, the only bifurcations in $G_{\swig(W)}$ with intermediate nodes in $W^o$ reduce to a single bidirected edge (the directed arms of the bifurcation cannot extend through $W^o$). So $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in (L_{\swig(W)})^{\sm W^o}$ iff $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L_{\swig(W)}$, i.e.\ iff $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L$ (since $\ul{v}^o = \ul{v}$ and $\ol{v}^o = \ol{v}$ for $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \notin W$). Under $\phi$ this matches $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L_{\doit(W)} = L \sm \{v \huh w \mid w \in W\}$, which equals $\ul{v} \huh \ol{v} \in L$ since $\ul{v}, \ol{v} \notin W$.

Since $\phi$ provides a bijection on nodes that preserves input/output status and all edges, the two CDMGs are isomorphic.

lean.name: CDMGNodeIso
lean.code:
-- Helper required to state the row: the cross-carrier graph isomorphism encoding the LN's `\cong`.
/-- A graph isomorphism between two CDMGs over potentially distinct
carrier types, encoded as a carrier *function* (not a carrier
bijection) together with the injectivity-on-nodes condition that
makes it a bijection on the actual node sets.

Concretely, a `CDMGNodeIso G H` packages:

* a function `toFun : ╬▒ Рєњ ╬▓`,
* a proof `injOn_nodes` that `toFun` is injective on
  `G.J Рѕф G.V` (so, together with the four image-equality fields
  below, it bijects `G`'s nodes onto `H.J Рѕф H.V`),
* four image equalities recording that `toFun` sends `G`'s four
  data fields `J / V / E / L` exactly to those of `H`. -/
structure CDMGNodeIso {╬▒ ╬▓ : Type u} (G : CDMG ╬▒) (H : CDMG ╬▓) where
  /-- The underlying function on carrier types. -/
  toFun : ╬▒ Рєњ ╬▓
  /-- The function is injective on `G`'s nodes. -/
  injOn_nodes : Set.InjOn toFun (G.J Рѕф G.V)
  /-- The function sends `G.J` to `H.J`. -/
  J_eq : H.J = toFun '' G.J
  /-- The function sends `G.V` to `H.V`. -/
  V_eq : H.V = toFun '' G.V
  /-- The function sends `G.E` to `H.E` (applied componentwise to
  endpoints). -/
  E_eq : H.E = (Prod.map toFun toFun) '' G.E
  /-- The function sends `G.L` to `H.L` (applied componentwise to
  endpoints). -/
  L_eq : H.L = (Prod.map toFun toFun) '' G.L

lean.name: hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs
lean.code:
/-- claim_3_19 -- `MarginalizingOutThe`. The hard intervention
`G.hardInterventionOn W` (LHS, over carrier `╬▒`) is
graph-isomorphic to the marginalization of the SWIG over the
`W^o = Sum.inl '' W` copies (RHS, over carrier `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW`). The
identification is `split1 W`, which sends `w Рѕѕ W` to
`Sum.inr РЪеw, hwРЪЕ` (the LN's `w Рєд w^i`) and is the identity on
`J Рѕф (V Рѕќ W)` (under the canonical `inl`-embedding, which is the
LN's `v Рєд v^o := v` for `v РѕЅ W`). -/
noncomputable def hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs
    {╬▒ : Type u} {G : CDMG ╬▒} {W : Set ╬▒} (hW : W Ріє G.V) :
    CDMGNodeIso (G.hardInterventionOn W)
                ((G.swig W hW).marginalize (Sum.inl '' W)) where
  toFun := split1 W
  injOn_nodes := fun _ _ _ _ hxy => split1_injective W hxy
  J_eq := by ...
  V_eq := by ...
  E_eq := by ...
  L_eq := by ...

lean_explanation:
The LN states the graph isomorphism $G_{\doit(W)} \cong (G_{\swig(W)})^{\setminus W^o}$ with witness $w \mapsto w^i$, for any CADMG $G = (J, V, E, L)$ and any subset of output nodes $W \subseteq V$. In Lean: `╬▒ : Type u` names the ambient vertex type (left implicit in the LN), `G : CDMG ╬▒` is the CADMG, `W : Set ╬▒` is the LN's $W$, and `hW : W Ріє G.V` discharges the LN's $W \subseteq V$ precondition. The hypothesis is load-bearing only on the SWIG side Рђћ both `hardInterventionOn` and `marginalize` accept any `W : Set ╬▒` regardless of $V$-containment, so it appears in the signature only because `G.swig W hW` demands it.

Two Mathlib idioms do real work. `Set.InjOn f S` is set-restricted injectivity (`f` is injective on `S`), exactly what a node-level bijection needs: injectivity is required only on the actual nodes `G.J Рѕф G.V`, not on the whole carrier `╬▒`. The notation `f '' S` is `Set.image f S` (the set `{f(x) | x Рѕѕ S}`). `Prod.map f f`, the canonical componentwise lift of a function to pairs, is how a carrier map acts on the edge sets `E` and `L`, both subsets of `╬▒ ├Ќ ╬▒`. The RHS carrier `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` (Lean's `Sum`, with `РєЉW` the subtype of elements of `W`) follows the convention pinned down in `NodeSplittingOn.lean`: `Sum.inl v` is the observation copy ($v^o$ in the LN) and `Sum.inr РЪеw, hwРЪЕ` is the fresh intervention-input copy ($w^i$). So the LN's $W^o$ is precisely `Sum.inl '' W : Set (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)`.

The statement bundles two declarations. `CDMGNodeIso G H` is a `structure` with one data field (`toFun : ╬▒ Рєњ ╬▓`) and five proof fields (a `Set.InjOn` plus four image-equalities for `J / V / E / L`); this captures the LN's $\cong$ between two CDMGs that may live over distinct carriers. `hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs` then constructs a `CDMGNodeIso` between `G.hardInterventionOn W` (the LN's $G_{\doit(W)}$) and `(G.swig W hW).marginalize (Sum.inl '' W)` (the LN's $(G_{\swig(W)})^{\setminus W^o}$). The witness carrier map is `split1 W : ╬▒ Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` (from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`): `v Рѕѕ W` maps to `Sum.inr РЪеv, hvРЪЕ` (the LN's $w \mapsto w^i$), and `v РѕЅ W` maps to `Sum.inl v` (the identity on $J \cup (V \setminus W)$ under the $v^o = v$ convention).
design_choices:
**The cross-carrier iso is encoded as a function `╬▒ Рєњ ╬▓` plus `Set.InjOn`, not a full carrier bijection `╬▒ РЅЃ ╬▓`.** The two CDMGs of this row live over carriers of *different cardinality* Рђћ LHS over `╬▒`, RHS over `╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW`. For any finite `╬▒` and non-empty `W`, no `╬▒ РЅЃ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` exists (the cardinalities differ by $|W|$), so an `Equiv`-shaped statement is literally unprovable in those cases. This is the central obstacle that rules out reusing the same-cardinality `CDMGEquiv` API of claim_3_7 / claim_3_10 (whose carrier bijections rebracket the *same* underlying carrier). The LN's $\cong$ is a *node*-level bijection, not a carrier-level one Рђћ it bijects $J \cup V$ on the LHS with $J' \cup V'$ on the RHS and says nothing about the unused parts of the carrier. `Set.InjOn toFun (G.J Рѕф G.V)` plus the four image-equalities for `J / V / E / L` captures exactly that, and no more.

**`CDMGNodeIso` is kept local to this file, not promoted to a shared module.** Single-consumer rationale: lifting now would force a placement decision Рђћ Section 3.1 alongside `CDMG.lean` (where the four-data-field shape is defined) or Section 3.2 alongside the same-cardinality `CDMGEquiv` Рђћ and possibly a unification with `CDMGEquiv`, both better deferred until a *second* cross-carrier-iso row reveals the natural shared API. The same wait-for-second-consumer policy already governs `CDMGEquiv` itself, and the cost of lifting later (re-import, possibly rename) is small compared to locking in the wrong shape now. No `refl` / `symm` / `trans` groupoid laws are written for the same reason: no chaining is yet on the table.

**The carrier map witness is `split1 W`, not a bespoke function.** `split1 W : ╬▒ Рєњ ╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW` (from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`) is exactly the LN's identification by construction: `v РѕЅ W` maps to `Sum.inl v` (the observation copy, identifying with the original vertex under the $v^o = v$ convention) and `v Рѕѕ W` maps to `Sum.inr РЪеv, hvРЪЕ` (the LN's $w \mapsto w^i$). The LN names only `w Рєд w^i` because the identity on the rest is "implicit by absence of any other rule"; in Lean the implicit rule is `Sum.inl`, which is precisely `split1`'s else-branch. Re-using `split1` removes a degree of freedom (any other equivalent encoding would be definitionally different and force `simp`-rewrites to convert) and aligns this row with the chapter-3 split-graph identifications used by claim_3_6 / claim_3_7 / claim_3_8 / claim_3_12 / def_3_12.

**$W^o$ is rendered as `Sum.inl '' W`, not `Sum.inr '' W` or the raw `W`.** Under the `Sum.inl = w^o` / `Sum.inr = w^i` convention from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`, the LN's $W^o$ Рђћ the $o$-copies of $W$ inside the SWIG's carrier $\alpha \oplus \uparrow W$ Рђћ is literally `Sum.inl '' W : Set (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)`. Marginalizing `Sum.inr '' W` (the $w^i$ copies) instead would be a *different* and *false* statement: deleting the fresh intervention inputs would collapse the SWIG back toward the observational graph $G$, not toward $G_{\doit(W)}$. Marginalizing the raw `W : Set ╬▒` is a type error Рђћ `marginalize` expects a `Set (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)`, not a `Set ╬▒`. So `Sum.inl '' W` is the unique correct choice.

**The conclusion uses $\cong$ (a `CDMGNodeIso`), not literal `=`.** The two CDMGs live over different carrier types Рђћ `CDMG ╬▒` versus `CDMG (╬▒ РіЋ РєЉW)` Рђћ so Lean's `=` between them is not even type-correct; an equality statement here would be a category error. The LN's $\cong$ is exactly the graph isomorphism captured by `CDMGNodeIso`. Contrast claim_3_4 / claim_3_8 / claim_3_13, where both sides of the chained equation live over the *same* carrier and the LN writes literal `=` (matched in Lean by an ordinary equality `theorem`).

**`hW : W Ріє G.V` appears at the signature level, but only because of the SWIG side.** `G.swig W hW` and `G.nodeSplittingOn W hW` carry `hW` as a precondition Рђћ load-bearing because without it the fresh split edge `(Sum.inl w, Sum.inr РЪеw, _РЪЕ)` would violate `V_subset` for `w Рѕѕ W Рѕќ G.V`. By contrast, `hardInterventionOn` and `marginalize` both *dropped* their LN preconditions because their constructions are well-defined for any `W`. So the LHS `G.hardInterventionOn W` does not need `hW`; only the RHS does, which is why the hypothesis appears in the signature.

**The declaration is a `noncomputable def`, not a `theorem`.** The conclusion type `CDMGNodeIso ..` is a `structure` (data, not a `Prop`), so `def` is the correct keyword. The `noncomputable` modifier propagates from the `split1` / `nodeSplittingOn` chain (both `noncomputable` because of the classical case-split on set membership). This matches the declaration style of `nodeSplittingOn_nodeSplittingOn_equiv` and `nodeSplittingOn_comm_equiv` in `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean`, where a `CDMGEquiv`-valued conclusion is similarly a `noncomputable def`.
